title: Ovilla perennis
taxonomy: Ovilla perennis is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is typically found growing in dry or rocky areas, including deserts and scrublands. Ovilla perennis is a small shrub with small white or purple flowers and has been used in traditional medicine. The classification of Ovilla perennis is further divided into the genus Ovilla and the order Celastrales.
uses: Ovilla perennis, also known as Wild Ovilla, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. Its roots have been employed to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are said to possess astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in soothing skin irritations and wounds. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Ovilla perennis as a medicinal agent. Additionally, its potential interactions with other medications and its effects on pregnant or breastfeeding individuals are not well-documented.
